findings.title: Unlimited gateway approvals persist and survive router delisting, enabling drains of proxy-held tokens

best_agent_match.title: Unlimited gateway approvals persist and can drain proxy-held tokens even after delisting

findings.title: Fee-on-transfer tokens are accounted by nominal input instead of actual receipt, allowing reserve drain

best_agent_match.title: Fee-on-transfer tokens can drain accumulated reserves because spending is based on nominal input, not actual receipt

findings.title: Configured per-token minimum and maximum route amounts are never enforced

best_agent_match.title: Configured per-token min/max amount limits are dead code

findings.title: Caller-controlled integrator address lets anyone impersonate discounted fee plans

best_agent_match.title: Unauthenticated integrator field lets anyone reuse discounted fee schedules

rejected_candidates.title: SmartApprove grants unlimited approvals to potentially malicious gateways
rejected_candidates.reason: Subsumed by F-001. The reportable issue is the persistent overbroad approval that remains live after use and after delisting, not a separate standalone bug.

rejected_candidates.title: No approval cleanup on router/gateway removal
rejected_candidates.reason: Subsumed by F-001 as part of the same sticky-approval root cause.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash loan attack vector via Uniswap V2
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited `FlawVerifier` is a local verification harness, not production protocol code. Flash liquidity is only part of an exploit path for other bugs, not a standalone vulnerability here.

rejected_candidates.title: No event emission for ERC20 approvals
rejected_candidates.reason: ERC20 approval state is already observable from token contracts; absence of an extra wrapper event is not a protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: sweepTokens allows admin to drain all tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an explicit admin power/centralization property, not an unintended vulnerability in the protocol logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero-address validation in sweepTokens
rejected_candidates.reason: `sweepTokens` is admin-only and delegates to `sendToken`; this does not create a realistic permissionless exploit or protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Fixed crypto fee underflow protection comment is misleading
rejected_candidates.reason: This is documentation quality, not a security issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Native routing emits unvalidated amount metadata
rejected_candidates.reason: The mismatch only affects off-chain interpretation of caller-supplied event fields; no concrete on-chain fund loss or permissionless protocol exploit is evidenced in the code.
